info.title: beerengineer_ Engine API
info.description:
HTTP surface for `@beerengineer/engine`. Canonical contract for every
client — CLI (remote mode), UI (when one exists), future chattool
webhooks. Hand-maintained; companion to `spec/api-contract.md`.

### Authentication

Normal localhost requests over loopback are admitted without token management.
Legacy callers may still send `x-beerengineer-token`, and non-loopback
callers can still use `BEERENGINEER_API_TOKEN` as an optional compatibility
secret, but the supported local operator flow does not require reading,
creating, exporting, or copying a token.

Inbound webhooks under `/webhooks/*` authenticate with channel-specific
secrets (e.g. `x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token`) and bypass the localhost
admission gate.

get.summary: Liveness and local DB reachability probe.

200.description: Engine is up and the local SQLite database is reachable.

503.description: Engine process answered but the local SQLite database probe failed.

get.summary: Workflow readiness probe for accepting engine work.
get.description: Reports unavailable until startup recovery has completed, during graceful shutdown, when the local DB probe fails, or when the lightweight worker-lease write sentinel cannot be updated. This endpoint does not check Git, LLM, workspace, setup, or Supabase readiness.

503.description: Engine process is alive but workflow work should not be accepted.

get.summary: Fetch this OpenAPI document as JSON.
Doctor report — environment readiness.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Limit the report to a known group id. Returns `400 unknown_group` if unrecognized.

get.summary: Read Git identity readiness.

200.description: Global or workspace Git identity readiness, including the engine-owned displayMode fact.

post.summary: Save beerengineer_ app-level Git identity.
Repair workspace-local Git identity using server-side workspace resolution.

200.description: Workspace repair applied and fresh readiness returned.

get.summary: Read-only updater status snapshot.

200.description: Current updater status, install paths, DB-path source, cached latest release, update-available flag, latest managed backup manifest if any, readiness/auth summary, and preflight summary.

get.summary: Fast updater preflight probe.

200.description: Whether the engine is idle and whether the update lock is held.

post.summary: Check the newest GitHub release against the current install and refresh the on-disk cache.

200.description: Release metadata for the latest GitHub release.

post.summary: Stage a GitHub release for update apply and queue the managed swap flow.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional retry key. When the same apply request is already queued or in flight, the server replays the original 202 body instead of staging a second update.

202.description: Release staged and apply attempt recorded. Managed installs continue asynchronously through shutdown/swap/restart after the response is flushed; unmanaged dev-checkout installs remain queued.

409.description: Update preflight refused the apply request.

post.summary: Reserved rollback endpoint.

409.description: Post-migration automatic rollback is not supported through this endpoint.

post.summary: Gracefully shut the engine down for an in-flight update operation.

202.description: Shutdown accepted; the engine will stop after flushing the response.

409.description: The provided operation id does not match the held update lock.

get.summary: List recent persisted update attempts, newest first.

200.description: Recent updater attempts and managed backup manifests, including succeeded-with-warning when restart health is good but non-core integration readiness failed.

get.summary: List registered workspaces.

post.summary: Register a workspace.

get.summary: Preflight check for a filesystem path before registering it.
get.description:
The **path** query parameter is an absolute filesystem path. The
response describes whether the directory exists, is writable, is a
git repo, looks greenfield, conflicts with existing registrations,
etc. Used by onboarding UIs to render warnings before the user
commits to `POST /workspaces`.

post.summary: Re-derive per-workspace config files from the registry.

get.summary: Fetch a registered workspace by key.

parameters.name: purge
parameters.description:
If set, also deletes the on-disk workspace directory (subject to
`allowedRoots`). Absent = registry-only removal.

post.summary: Resolve a workspace's on-disk root (server-side open).

get.summary: Read workspace Supabase execution-readiness state.

post.summary: Connect a workspace to a Supabase project resolved from the route key.

200.description: Project connection saved and readiness returned.

post.summary: Rotate the Supabase Management API token used by readiness checks.
Create or attach a workspace persistent Supabase test branch.

200.description: Persistent branch setup attempted and readiness returned.

get.summary: List items, optionally filtered.

post.summary: Create an implementation item from a prepared local directory.

404.description: Workflow Git readiness blocked or workspace-related lookup failed.

get.summary: Fetch a single item.
Fetch visual-companion wireframe artifact for an item.
Fetch frontend-design artifact for an item.

post.summary: Perform a named action on an item.

oneOf.description: Required for import_prepared; local directory readable by the engine process.

404.description: Item not found or workflow Git readiness blocked.

409.description: Invalid transition, conflicting state, or workflow Git readiness blocked.

422.description: Operation rejected by domain rules or workflow Git readiness blocked.

get.summary: List runs.
get.description: Returns all runs, newest first. The handler currently ignores any query parameters; filters (workspace, itemId, status, limit, cursor) will be added when a concrete client needs them.

post.summary: Start a new run from a title/description.

get.summary: Fetch a run. Includes `openPrompt` when waiting on a user answer.
Run + all its stage runs.
Recovery detail for a blocked/failed run.

post.summary: Retry a retained diagnosis branch through the existing run recovery path.

200.description: Run recovery was accepted on the retained branch.

409.description: Conflict — the run is no longer eligible for retained-branch retry or another resume blocker applies.

post.summary: Clear a retained diagnosis branch and continue recovery on the fresh path.

200.description: Run recovery was accepted on the fresh path after clearing the retained branch.

400.description: Bad Request — capability blocked or missing readiness.

409.description: Conflict — the run is no longer eligible for clear-and-fresh or another resume blocker applies.

post.summary: Resume a blocked/failed run with an operator remediation summary.

409.description: Conflict — generic resume blocker or an explicit retained-diagnosis operator decision.

post.summary: Regenerate and safely replace the active plan for an inactive run.

409.description: Replan rejected because the run is still active or does not yet have a persisted plan.

post.summary: Retry the same run after Supabase readiness setup has been updated.

200.description: Run resumed from the Supabase readiness blocker.

409.description: Conflict — generic resume blocker or an explicit retained-diagnosis operator decision.

get.summary: Operator-facing transcript (system/message/question/answer).

post.summary: Answer an open prompt. Returns the post-write conversation.

get.summary: Projected event log for a run, filtered by messaging level.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Stable `MessageEntry.id` cursor. Omit for head of log.

post.summary: Record a free-form user message into a run (does **not** answer a prompt).

get.summary: Server-Sent Events stream for a single run.
List artifact files produced by a run.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path relative to the run's workspace directory. Traversal (`..`) is stripped.

get.summary: Fetch a single artifact file.
Server-Sent Events stream scoped to a workspace (or all).
Board view (column/row aggregate) for a workspace.
Preview info for an item's managed worktree.

post.summary: Start the local preview command for an item's managed worktree.

200.description: Preview start accepted or detected as already running.

post.summary: Stop the local preview command for an item's managed worktree.

200.description: Preview stop accepted or already stopped.

get.summary: Recent outbound notification deliveries (audit log).

post.summary: Emit a test notification on a configured channel.
Inbound Telegram chat-tool webhook.
post.description:
Authenticates with `x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token`. Bypasses the
engine's CSRF gate. Not intended to be called by internal clients.

post.summary: Initialize app config, data directory, and database state.

200.description: Initialization succeeded or was already complete.

get.summary: Read the effective setup/configuration view.

parameters.name: workspaceKey
parameters.description: Optional workspace key. When provided, Telegram inbound setup resolves effective workspace overrides plus field-level provenance.

200.description: Effective setup/config view with secret references only.

404.description: Workspace key was provided but no matching workspace exists.

207.description: Patch partially accepted; rejected fields explain failures.

409.description: Setup config does not exist yet; initialize first.

post.summary: Register or update the Telegram webhook against the effective setup scope.

parameters.name: workspaceKey
parameters.description: Optional workspace key. When provided, resolves effective Telegram overrides for that workspace before calling Telegram.

200.description: Telegram webhook matches the configured callback URL.

400.description: Local validation failed, Telegram rejected the request, or the resulting provider state did not match the expected callback URL.

404.description: Workspace key was provided but no matching workspace exists.

post.summary: Start a live Telegram verification round-trip for the effective setup scope.

parameters.name: workspaceKey
parameters.description: Optional workspace key. When provided, resolves effective Telegram overrides for that workspace before starting live verification.

200.description: Live verification was started and is now pending a matching Telegram reply.

400.description: Baseline setup is not ready or Telegram rejected the outbound verification message.

404.description: Workspace key was provided but no matching workspace exists.

post.summary: Run setup checks again, optionally for one group.

get.summary: Read redacted metadata for a stored secret reference.

post.summary: Replace, disable, reactivate, delete, or test a secret.

409.description: Secret test failed or was rejected.

501.description: No tester is registered for this secret.

post.summary: Validate and rotate the Supabase Management API token.

patch.summary: Update workspace Supabase cleanup and protection settings.

post.summary: Attach an existing Supabase project to a workspace.
Recreate the configured persistent Supabase test branch after typed confirmation.
Disconnect Supabase project metadata from a workspace.

get.summary: Get merge gate status

post.summary: Retry Supabase branch validation
Destroy a stored Supabase run branch after typed confirmation.

code.description: One of `bad_request`, `not_found`, `forbidden`, `invalid_transition`, `prompt_not_open`, `run_blocked`, and a few route-specific codes.

leaseWrite.description: Result of the lightweight readiness sentinel write. `skipped` means an earlier readiness precondition already failed.

effectiveWorkerCap.description: Session-wide worker admission cap derived at startup from the explicit override or the host-memory default.

kind.description: `started` for run-creating actions, `transitioned` / `completed` / etc. for pure state actions.

readiness.description: Workspace Git readiness snapshot returned by the engine setup git-identity domain.

recovery_user_message.description: Projected user-facing recovery message. Present for lost-worker recoveries; null for other recovery causes. Derived from recovery status/cause/summary, not stored as a DB column.

execution_harness_selections.description: Projected resolved execution-role selections for operator visibility. Each entry identifies the execution role plus its harness/runtime/provider/model.

answerTo.description: Present iff `kind === 'answer'`; the promptId being answered.

branchRef.description: The retained branch currently blocking resume when the provider exposed one.

recovery_user_message.description: Projected user-facing recovery message to display before generic fallback copy when present.

currentStage.description: Engine stage key for the item's authoritative run, or null when no live stage. Drives the mini-stepper on the board.

recovery_user_message.description: Projected user-facing recovery message for the latest run on the card. Clients should prefer this before generic fallback copy.

meta.description: Display-friendly key-value tags for the card. Currently: phase, projects.

oneOf.description: Workspace has no Supabase context and no destructive findings — gate panel suppressed in UI.
